metadata.first_question_preview: In genetic algorithms, representation via permutations
metadata.assignment_note: Inferred from segment count/content because translated all-in-one TXT contains no filename headers.

questions.question: In genetic algorithms, representation via permutations

answers.a: Does not allow the use of recombination operators
answers.b: Does not allow the use of mutation operators
answers.c: Does not allow more than two genes with the same value in a chromosome

questions.question: In an evolutionary algorithm, the fitness function: 1. Evaluates the quality of the algorithm; 2. Must be selected randomly; 3. Evaluates the speed of finding the solution relative to resource consumption; 4. Must be modified at every iteration; 5. Selects the individuals that will reproduce; 6. Selects the individuals that pass to the next generation; 7. Evaluates the quality of the current population compared to the initial population; 8. Evaluates the quality of the current population compared to the previous generation; 9. Evaluates the quality of each candidate.
Let X=[ 6 1 8 10 5 7 9 3 4 2 ] and Y=[ 9 8 7 3 6 1 5 10 4 2 ] be permutations. Which offspring are generated by using the CX operator?

answers.c: The most used mutation operator is swap mutation

questions.question: In a genetic algorithm, the survivor selection operation: 1. Is applied to the current population; 2. In some variants requires the calculation of a selection probability distribution; 3. Always uses random factors; 4. Chooses the next generation from individuals available after the mutation operation; 5. The chosen individuals are always feasible; 6. Sometimes uses random factors; 7. Ensures the perpetuation of the individual with maximum quality from the current population; 8. Leads to an increase in the average quality of the current population; 9. Guarantees obtaining a generation with superior average quality if age-based selection is used; 10. Is applied at the beginning of each iteration; 11. Is applied to the offspring obtained from the current population; 12. Is applied to the current population and to the offspring obtained from the current population.
